schemaVersion: Ehel English Course Final Assessment v1.2
subject: English
title: Grade 6 English Final Course Quiz
description: A cumulative assessment of vocabulary, reading, language and communication across Units 1-9.

sections.title: Words and Meaning
sections.description: Use Grade 6 vocabulary accurately in familiar and new contexts.

sections.title: Reading and Language
sections.description: Show reading understanding and control of Grade 6 language patterns.

sections.title: Communication and Application
sections.description: Apply English to speaking, writing and purposeful communication.

questions.quizTitle: There's a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'illegal' mean?
questions.options: A tool or device used to make music or do a specific job | Gave or provided something that was needed | Not allowed by law or rules | The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
questions.correctAnswer: Not allowed by law or rules
questions.explanation: It is illegal to steal someone else's property.

questions.quizTitle: There's a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'Christian' mean?
questions.options: A person who follows the teachings of Jesus Christ | A pleasing combination of things that work well together; peace | A tool or device used to make music or do a specific job | Gave or provided something that was needed
questions.correctAnswer: A person who follows the teachings of Jesus Christ
questions.explanation: My friend is a Christian and goes to church every Sunday.

questions.quizTitle: There's a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'entry' mean?
questions.options: A pleasing combination of things that work well together; peace | A way of looking at or thinking about something | Gave or provided something that was needed | The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
questions.correctAnswer: The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
questions.explanation: You need a ticket for entry to the concert.

questions.quizTitle: There's a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'instrument' mean?
questions.options: A pleasing combination of things that work well together; peace | A tool or device used to make music or do a specific job | A way of looking at or thinking about something | To remove or hide parts of something considered inappropriate
questions.correctAnswer: A tool or device used to make music or do a specific job
questions.explanation: She plays the piano, which is a musical instrument.

questions.question: What does 'geographic' mean?
questions.options: A person who speaks for God or tells what will happen in the future | Related to the physical features of the Earth and how places are arranged | The ability to create ideas or pictures in your mind | The ability to make choices and take action
questions.correctAnswer: Related to the physical features of the Earth and how places are arranged
questions.explanation: The geographic features of our country include mountains, rivers, and valleys.

questions.question: What does 'originally' mean?
questions.options: A person who speaks for God or tells what will happen in the future | At the beginning or in the first place | The ability to make choices and take action | To become worse or to decline in quality
questions.correctAnswer: At the beginning or in the first place
questions.explanation: Originally, the story was written as a book before it became a movie.

questions.question: What does 'imagination' mean?
questions.options: A person who speaks for God or tells what will happen in the future | Doing something in a way that saves time and energy | The ability to create ideas or pictures in your mind | To become worse or to decline in quality
questions.correctAnswer: The ability to create ideas or pictures in your mind
questions.explanation: With her imagination, she can turn a simple box into a spaceship.

questions.question: What does 'agency' mean?
questions.options: Actions or words that encourage people to go against their government or authority | Doing something in a way that saves time and energy | The ability to make choices and take action | To become worse or to decline in quality
questions.correctAnswer: The ability to make choices and take action
questions.explanation: In our class, we have the agency to choose our own projects.

questions.question: What does 'threat' mean?
questions.options: A difficult situation that causes suffering or trouble. | A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others. | Something that can cause harm or danger. | To treat someone in a cruel or unfair way, making them feel weak or powerless.
questions.correctAnswer: Something that can cause harm or danger.
questions.explanation: The dark clouds are a threat of a coming storm.

questions.question: What does 'treacherous' mean?
questions.options: A difficult situation that causes suffering or trouble. | Dangerous and not safe, often because it is not what it seems. | To treat someone badly or unfairly because of their beliefs or identity. | To treat someone in a cruel or unfair way, making them feel weak or powerless.
questions.correctAnswer: Dangerous and not safe, often because it is not what it seems.
questions.explanation: The path through the mountains was treacherous after the rains.

questions.question: What does 'dictator' mean?
questions.options: A difficult situation that causes suffering or trouble. | A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others. | To say that something is not allowed. | To treat someone badly or unfairly because of their beliefs or identity.
questions.correctAnswer: A leader who has complete power and makes all the rules without asking others.
questions.explanation: The dictator did not allow people to speak freely or choose their leaders.

questions.question: What does 'oppress' mean?
questions.options: To keep going through something difficult without giving up. | To say that something is not allowed. | To treat someone badly or unfairly because of their beliefs or identity. | To treat someone in a cruel or unfair way, making them feel weak or powerless.
questions.correctAnswer: To treat someone in a cruel or unfair way, making them feel weak or powerless.
questions.explanation: The soldiers tried to oppress the villagers by taking away their rights.

questions.question: What does 'straits' mean?
questions.options: A group of people travelling together, often across a desert. | A narrow body of water between two pieces of land. | A short trip or journey, usually for fun or learning. | An area of land that belongs to a person, group, or country.
questions.correctAnswer: A narrow body of water between two pieces of land.
questions.explanation: The ships passed through the straits to get to the other side of the island.

questions.question: What does 'explorer' mean?
questions.options: A group of people travelling together, often across a desert. | A person who travels to new places to discover things. | An area of land that belongs to a person, group, or country. | The process of finding your way from one place to another.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who travels to new places to discover things.
questions.explanation: The explorer found a new island in the ocean.

questions.question: What does 'excursion' mean?
questions.options: A group of people living together with their own culture, laws, and way of life. | A short trip or journey, usually for fun or learning. | An area of land that belongs to a person, group, or country. | The process of finding your way from one place to another.
questions.correctAnswer: A short trip or journey, usually for fun or learning.
questions.explanation: Our class went on an excursion to the science museum.

questions.question: What does 'caravan' mean?
questions.options: A group of people living together with their own culture, laws, and way of life. | A group of people travelling together, often across a desert. | A scientist who studies how things move and work in the universe. | The process of finding your way from one place to another.
questions.correctAnswer: A group of people travelling together, often across a desert.
questions.explanation: We travelled across the country in our caravan, stopping at different campsites.

questions.question: What does 'humble' mean?
questions.options: Easily affected by things around you, like feelings or physical things. | In a strong and steady way; not easily moved or changed. | Not enough; very small in amount or quality. | Not thinking that you are better than others; modest.
questions.correctAnswer: Not thinking that you are better than others; modest.
questions.explanation: Even though he won the race, he remained humble and thanked his friends for their support.

questions.question: What does 'eagerly' mean?
questions.options: Easily affected by things around you, like feelings or physical things. | Feeling uneasy or not relaxed. | In a strong and steady way; not easily moved or changed. | In a way that shows you really want to do something.
questions.correctAnswer: In a way that shows you really want to do something.
questions.explanation: She eagerly opened her birthday presents.

questions.question: What does 'meager' mean?
questions.options: Easily affected by things around you, like feelings or physical things. | Feeling uneasy or not relaxed. | Not enough; very small in amount or quality. | Something that is not certain or may not be true.
questions.correctAnswer: Not enough; very small in amount or quality.
questions.explanation: The meal was meager, with only a small piece of bread and a little bit of soup.

questions.question: What does 'firmly' mean?
questions.options: Feeling uneasy or not relaxed. | Feeling upset because you cannot do something you want to do. | In a strong and steady way; not easily moved or changed. | Something that is not certain or may not be true.
questions.correctAnswer: In a strong and steady way; not easily moved or changed.
questions.explanation: She held the rope firmly so it would not slip from her hands.

questions.question: What does 'wisdom' mean?
questions.options: A spoken or written explanation or discussion about something. | The ability to make good choices and decisions based on knowledge and experience. | The act of watching something carefully to learn more about it. | The written words in a book, article, or message.
questions.correctAnswer: The ability to make good choices and decisions based on knowledge and experience.
questions.explanation: My grandmother has a lot of wisdom because she has lived for many years and learned many things.

questions.question: What does 'script' mean?
questions.options: A short description or title that explains a picture or a video. | A spoken or written explanation or discussion about something. | A written text for a play, movie, or show that tells what the characters say and do. | The act of watching something carefully to learn more about it.
questions.correctAnswer: A written text for a play, movie, or show that tells what the characters say and do.
questions.explanation: The actors read their lines from the script during the rehearsal.

questions.question: What does 'text' mean?
questions.options: A short description or title that explains a picture or a video. | Numbers or shapes that represent something. | The act of watching something carefully to learn more about it. | The written words in a book, article, or message.
questions.correctAnswer: The written words in a book, article, or message.
questions.explanation: I read the text in my English book to learn new words.

questions.question: What does 'commentary' mean?
questions.options: A short description or title that explains a picture or a video. | A spoken or written explanation or discussion about something. | Numbers or shapes that represent something. | Permission to do something.
questions.correctAnswer: A spoken or written explanation or discussion about something.
questions.explanation: The teacher gave a commentary on the story we read in class.

questions.question: What does 'conduction' mean?
questions.options: The layer of air above the weather layer, where planes fly and the ozone layer is found. | The process of digging in the ground to find things like fossils or old buildings. | The process of using machines to do work instead of people. | The way heat or electricity moves through a material.
questions.correctAnswer: The way heat or electricity moves through a material.
questions.explanation: When you touch a metal spoon in a hot pot, the heat travels through the spoon by conduction.

questions.question: What does 'pollination' mean?
questions.options: A picture or drawing that helps explain something. | The process of digging in the ground to find things like fossils or old buildings. | The process of transferring pollen from one flower to another to help plants make seeds. | The process of using machines to do work instead of people.
questions.correctAnswer: The process of transferring pollen from one flower to another to help plants make seeds.
questions.explanation: Bees help with pollination by moving pollen between flowers.

questions.question: What does 'stratosphere' mean?
questions.options: A picture or drawing that helps explain something. | An economic system where people own businesses and make money by selling goods and services. | The layer of air above the weather layer, where planes fly and the ozone layer is found. | The process of using machines to do work instead of people.
questions.correctAnswer: The layer of air above the weather layer, where planes fly and the ozone layer is found.
questions.explanation: The airplane flew high in the stratosphere, above the clouds.

questions.question: What does 'excavation' mean?
questions.options: A person who uses money to make more money by starting businesses or investing. | A picture or drawing that helps explain something. | An economic system where people own businesses and make money by selling goods and services. | The process of digging in the ground to find things like fossils or old buildings.
questions.correctAnswer: The process of digging in the ground to find things like fossils or old buildings.
questions.explanation: The workers started the excavation to find ancient artefacts buried in the ground.

questions.question: What does 'ritual' mean?
questions.options: A person who follows a religion that is not one of the main world religions, often believing in many gods. | A special way of doing something that is done regularly or at certain times. | A time when something becomes popular or important again. | Lasting forever; never ending.
questions.correctAnswer: A special way of doing something that is done regularly or at certain times.
questions.explanation: Every morning, my family has a ritual of eating breakfast together.

questions.question: What does 'disciple' mean?
questions.options: A member of the Christian church that is led by the Pope. | A person who follows a religion that is not one of the main world religions, often believing in many gods. | A person who follows and learns from a teacher or leader. | A time when something becomes popular or important again.
questions.correctAnswer: A person who follows and learns from a teacher or leader.
questions.explanation: The disciple listened carefully to the wise words of his teacher.
